title: Multi-part build support via .parts files
description: Makefile discovers .parts files to build multi-part .scad models. foo.scad + foo.parts -> out/foo-<part>.stl for each part.
close_reason: script/build handles .scad (single STL) and .parts (per-part STLs) with PART= override

title: Pre-commit hook to verify .scad files compile
description: Add a pre-commit script that builds all .scad files and fails the commit if any don't compile.
close_reason: Pre-commit hook wired via script/hooks/pre-commit, installed by script/bootstrap

title: CLI build system for OpenSCAD files
description: Set up a build system to compile .scad files to .stl. Pattern: %.scad -> out/%.stl
close_reason: Makefile (%.scad -> out/%.stl) with check/clean targets, script/build wrapper, and pre-commit hook

title: Gridfinity bin for Kiprim LD50E laser distance measurer

comments.author: Björn Andersson
comments.text: Got a full print and it's a tad too snug for me to be comfortable giving it to anyone else. Need to adjust it by 1-2mm and print another cut bin to verify it sits nicely

comments.author: Björn Andersson
comments.text:
Did another test print that I cut to size and it seems good. Doing a full print. Realized that I had confused myself with the measurements last time and most likely the model had been updated in the .3mf file, just hard to understand because git sees it as a binary (since it's a zip) and I could've seen the diff if it could read and understand the format better.

Wonder if there's any special differs that could handle that...?

title: Manage vendor dependencies
close_reason: Added dependencies.txt (repo url + SHA + target dir format) and script/bootstrap to clone/fetch and checkout pinned versions. vendor/ gitignored. Chose vendor/ as standard convention.

title: Research: multi-color OpenSCAD models in Bambu Studio
description: Research available approaches for handling multi-color OpenSCAD models when printing with Bambu Studio and AMS. Understand what OpenSCAD can export natively, what the slicer supports, and what the practical workflow should be.
design: Investigated multiple approaches: separate bodies (normal parts), modifier meshes, OpenSCAD native 3MF export, and ColorSCAD external tool. See notes for findings.
notes:
## Findings (April 2026)

### What works: separate STLs imported as modifiers
Export the full model as ONE body (cup with lip included) and export color regions (lip ring, floor slabs) as separate STLs. In Bambu Studio, import color region STLs as modifier meshes (not normal parts) and assign them a different extruder. Modifiers change filament color within the existing body's geometry without adding perimeters.

### What doesn't work: separate bodies (normal parts)
Importing color regions as normal parts causes the slicer to generate independent perimeters for each body. This creates a visible proud seam on the outer wall even when the body boundary is 5mm+ inside the cup. The slicer generates extra outer wall segments and massive extrusion spikes (13x normal) at body boundary layers. G-code analysis confirmed the outer wall positions are identical at every layer, but thermal/pressure effects from the extra extrusion cause physical bulging.

### What doesn't work yet: OpenSCAD native 3MF color export
OpenSCAD nightly builds (2025+) can export multi-body 3MF using color() + lazy-union (PRs #5527, #5577). BUT Bambu Studio uses non-standard 3MF color attributes and ignores spec-compliant ones. See: github.com/bambulab/BambuStudio/issues/4292, github.com/openscad/openscad/issues/6588

### What exists but untested: ColorSCAD
github.com/jschobben/colorscad — external tool that runs OpenSCAD once per color and merges into multi-body 3MF. Works with stable OpenSCAD.

### CGAL non-manifold issue
Never subtract two nearly-identical solids (e.g. cup_with_lip - cup_no_lip). CGAL produces non-manifold edges from coincident faces. Use library dedicated modules (cupLip() from module_lip.scad) or intersection().

### Practical workflow
1. OpenSCAD: export cup (full body with lip), lip (modifier shape), floor (modifier shape) as separate STLs
2. Bambu Studio: import cup as main body, import lip and floor as modifier meshes
3. Assign different extruders to modifiers
4. Slice and print — no body boundaries, no extra perimeters, no seam artifacts
close_reason: Research complete. Key finding: use modifier meshes in the slicer, not separate bodies. Separate bodies create perimeter artifacts. OpenSCAD native 3MF export exists but Bambu Studio doesn't support it. Documented practical workflow in notes.

title: Explore automated 3MF update workflow
description:
Investigate ways to automatically update Bambu Studio 3MF files when OpenSCAD models change, preserving print settings (filament assignments, transforms, plate position).

## Approaches explored

### 1. Patch-based
Export fresh 3MF from OpenSCAD, apply a saved diff to add print settings. Problem: mesh data changes invalidate the patch on any geometry change.

### 2. Template 3MF with mesh injection
Keep a template 3MF with everything except mesh data (Metadata/, config, UUIDs, filament assignments, transforms). Build script injects fresh mesh data into 3D/Objects/*.model XML. Template is small and version-controllable.

### 3. STL-to-3MF XML conversion
Build script converts STL binary output to 3MF XML vertices/triangles format and swaps mesh sections in an existing 3MF, preserving UUIDs and settings.

### 4. Reload from Disk (current workflow)
Bambu Studio can right-click a part > Reload from Disk to swap geometry while preserving all settings. Zero engineering cost but manual.

## 3MF structure notes
- 3MF is a ZIP archive
- 3D/3dmodel.model — assembly XML, references parts by UUID with transforms
- 3D/Objects/*.model — mesh data as XML vertices + triangles (same data as STL)
- Metadata/ — slicer settings, filament assignments, thumbnails
- UUIDs (p:UUID) identify parts and stay consistent across reloads
- Schema: http://schemas.microsoft.com/3dmanufacturing/core/2015/02

value: G-code debugging for print artifacts: Compare extrusion volume per layer across transitions. Count outer wall segments (extra = multi-body boundary issue). Check layer times (dramatic drops cause cooling artifacts). Verify outer wall X/Y positions are consistent. Look for unexpected features (Top surface, Gap infill) at transitions.

Multi-color OpenSCAD + Bambu Studio: Export the full model as ONE body (e.g. gridfinity_cup with lip included). Export color regions (lip, floor) as separate STLs/PARTs. In Bambu Studio, import as separate parts within the same object and assign different extruders. For the gridfinity lip, use cupLip() from module_lip.scad to render just the lip ring — never subtract two nearly-identical solids (cup_with_lip - cup_no_lip) as CGAL produces non-manifold edges from coincident faces.

Hull lines (thermal contraction artifacts): When a model transitions from dense to sparse cross-section (e.g. solid fill to recess), the cooling mass contracts and pulls on the outer wall, creating a visible line. Fix: add thermal relief voids (air gap rings) inside the wall at transition Z heights. Parameters: 1mm width, 5mm height, inset 3mm from outer wall (less where walls are thin like the lip). Use rounded corners on the relief ring. Adaptive cubic infill is better than lightning — lightning leaves narrow wall zones empty.

STL validation: OpenSCAD reporting manifold NoError is necessary but not sufficient. Always check the slicer's mesh validation too — Bambu Studio will warn about non-manifold edges that OpenSCAD missed.

Always use dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true when running script/build or OpenSCAD commands. OpenSCAD Qt framework crashes inside the Claude Code sandbox with 'Incompatible processor / neon'. Project settings have excludedCommands so the flag doesn't require user approval.
